heading: Expressions
body:
A unary expression is an expression that takes a single argument.
In MongoDB Search, you can use unary expressions to calculate the log10(x)
or log10(x+1) of a specified number. For example, you can use
`log1p` to influence the relevance score by document popularity
score. It must include one of the following options:

| Option | Type       | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------ | ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| log    | expression | Calculates the log10 of a number. For example:\{ "function": \{ "log": \{ "multiply": \[ \{"path": "popularity"}, \{"constant": 0.5}, \{"score": "relevance"} ] } } }In the previous example, MongoDB Search calculates the log10 of the arithmetic expression that uses the multiply option to calculate the product of the numeric value of the popularity field, the constant value of 0.5, and the relevance score assigned by MongoDB Search.If the expression specified for the log option evaluates to less than or equal to 0, then the log evaluates to undefined. For example:\{ "function": \{ "log": \{ "constant": -5.1 } } }In the previous example, MongoDB Search evaluates the the log10 of the constant expression -5.1 to undefined. Therefore, the final score of the document is 0. |
| log1p  | expression | Adds 1 to the number and then calculates its log10. For example:\{ "function": \{ "log1p": \{ "path": \{ "value": "rating", "undefined": 4 } } } }In the previous example, MongoDB Search adds 1 to the expression that uses the path option to evaluate to the numeric value of the rating field, then calculates the log10 of the resulting sum.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
